chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.S- 1755 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For Katcha Peshi. 17.11.2016. None present for the petitioner. Mr. Ali Abbas Memon, State Counsel alongwith SIP Muhammad Changal on bhelaf of SSP Hyderabad, ASI Sikander on behalf of SHO Qasimabad and ASI Atta Muhammad Kaka, Police Line Hyderabad. -.-.-. On last date of hearing a restraining order was passed against Respondents and in particular Respondent No.4-ASI Atta Muhammad Kaka, who is also present in Court and states that he has not acted at the behest of private respondents. According to Statements filed by the learned State Counsel on behalf of the official respondents, amongst private respondents and petitioner there is civil litigation going on with regard to a portion of common land between private respondents and petitioner. The same has been confirmed in Paragraph-11 of the petition as well that a 3rd Class Suit No. 153 of 2011is sub judice before the concerned Court. Be that as it may, the police officials are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner and will provide him protection if they are approached by the petitioner with such request. Looking at the facts of the case, it is further directed that respondent No.4-ASI Atta Muhammad Kaka will serve the copy of the order passed today on Kashif and Habibullah, private respondents No.5 & 6, respectively, who are further restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner and if they have any grievance, the same can be redressed either through above mentioned suit or by invoking the due process of law.

Be that as it may, the police officials are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner and will provide him protection if they are approached by the petitioner with such request. Looking at the facts of the case, it is further directed that respondent No.4-ASI Atta Muhammad Kaka will serve the copy of the order passed today on Kashif and Habibullah, private respondents No.5 & 6, respectively, who are further restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner and if they have any grievance, the same can be redressed either through above mentioned suit or by invoking the due process of law. With these observations and directions, instant Constitutional Petition stands disposed of. Copy of this order be provided to learned State Counsel for compliance. JUDGE
